channel.title: NYT > Style
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Unforgettable Oscars Looks 2026: Bumblebee Brooches, Jewel-Tone Gowns and More
item.description: Jewel-tone gowns, bumblebee brooches, overalls and more.
item.dc:creator: The Styles Desk
item.media:credit: Daniel Cole/Reuters
item.media:description: Jessie Buckley packed a one-two punch in two-tone Chanel.

item.title: Vanity Fair Oscars After-Party: See the Red Carpet Looks
item.description: The magazine’s new editor slashed the guest list in hopes of increasing the event’s starriness per capita. Here’s what those who made the cut wore to party at LACMA.
item.dc:creator: The Styles Desk
item.media:credit: Neilson Barnard/Getty Images
item.media:description: Jessie Buckley won the Oscar for best actress for “Hamnet.”

item.title: Mardi Gras Indians Keep a 150-Year Tradition Alive in New Orleans
item.description: On Super Sunday, a 150-year-old tradition of painstaking craftsmanship is put on display in New Orleans’ streets with suits made of delicate beads and billowing ostrich feathers.
item.dc:creator: Shannon Sims and Emily Kask
item.media:description: Alphonse Robair, a veteran chief of the Black Hatchet tribe in New Orleans’ Ninth Ward who goes by Dowee, putting on his Mardi Gras Indian suit.

item.title: Oscars Eve in Beverly Hills With Teyana Taylor, Jeff Bezos and Nicole Kidman
item.description: Film’s top stars buzzed over lychee martinis at Chanel’s annual Polo Lounge bash, and a Netflix chief mused about Hollywood’s biggest drama of the year.
item.dc:creator: Callie Holtermann and Nina Westervelt

item.title: After 17 Oscar Nominations, Diane Warren Is Still Chasing Her First Win
item.description: For Diane Warren, the work is the reward. But after 16 losses, an original song Oscar would be nice, too.
item.dc:creator: Kellina Moore
item.media:credit: Nina Westervelt for The New York Times
item.media:description: Diane Warren this year received her 17th Oscar nomination without a single win. “I’ve never taken awards as validation,” she said. “The work is validation.”

item.title: Alas, You Will Never Look Like J.F.K. Jr. in Your Chinos
item.description: Recent marketing campaigns and TikToks reflect the craze for “Love Story.” They show that the Kennedy archetype still can’t be purchased.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Gallagher
item.category: Love Story: John F. Kennedy Jr. & Carolyn Bessette (TV Program)

item.title: The Documentary Filmmaker Styling Her Peers for the Oscars
item.description: Rachel Beth Anderson knows firsthand that her fellow documentarians can rarely afford to dress for awards shows. So she started a concierge service designed to help them.
item.dc:creator: Lux Alptraum and Gabby Jones
item.media:description: Ms. Anderson and Ms. Blair fit Mr. Borenstein in a cream-colored Yves Saint Laurent suit, which he initially worried might be more appropriate for the director of “a fun vampire movie.”

item.title: Fans Try to Decode Lily Allen’s Dress. The Designer Says There’s More to It.
item.description: The set designer of Ms. Allen’s “West End Girl” tour talks about the singer’s receipt dress.
item.dc:creator: Yola Mzizi

item.title: The Good List: 6 Things to Add Delight to Your Day
item.description: Peony tulips, transcendent eye contact and a new game from the creator of Wordle.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Kirsch
item.media:credit: John Gall

item.title: Tiny Love Stories: ‘My Friends Said That He Was Too Old for Me’
item.description: Modern Love in miniature, featuring reader-submitted stories of no more than 100 words.
item.media:credit: Brian Rea

item.title: At SXSW With the First-Time Filmmakers Behind ‘Sparks’
item.description: Fergus Campbell, Lola Lafia and a merry band of Zoomers dazzled at their first film festival with an affecting debut about friendship, time travel and queer identity.
item.dc:creator: Emma Schartz and Lauren Withrow
item.category: South by Southwest Music and Media Conference
item.media:description: Actors who make up “the Crop,” from left: Madison Hu, Charlie B. Foster, Elsie Fisher, Denny McAuliffe and Simon Downes Toney.

item.title: My Relative Takes Forever to Reply to My Texts. What Can I Do?
item.description: Stung by a family member’s laggardly response times, a reader considers confronting the issue head-on: “Do you make all your friends wait like this?”
item.dc:creator: Philip Galanes
item.media:credit: Miguel Porlan

item.title: Plus-Size Southwest Passengers Say New Extra-Seat Policy Has Led to Fat Shaming
item.description: Since the airline changed its policy on larger passengers this year, travelers say agents have publicly scrutinized their bodies and made them buy extra seats.
item.dc:creator: Christine Chung

item.title: Oscars Red Carpet as a Floor Upgrade? Woman’s Dumpster Dive Led to a New Rug.
item.description: Paige Thalia’s apartment floor got an upgrade this week thanks to some leftovers from the Academy Awards.
item.dc:creator: Madison Malone Kircher

item.title: How Fashion Designers From Tom Ford, Hermes and More Bid Farewell This Season
item.description: How designers took their bows this season.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Gallagher
item.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: Street Style Look of the Week: Plaid Wool Sweater and Pants
item.description: Electric colors and a soft texture distinguished an ensemble seen on the streets of London.
item.dc:creator: Simbarashe Cha

item.title: Why Do Men in the Trump Administration Wear Shoes That Are Too Big?
item.description: It’s an affliction — and not one relegated to the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Gallagher
item.media:credit: Chip Somodevilla/Getty Images
item.media:description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio, seen here in January with shoes that have a visible gap at the back.

item.title: John Galliano Returns to Fashion With Zara Partnership
item.description: The designer is embarking on a partnership with Zara.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman
item.media:credit: Jacopo Raule/Getty Images
item.media:description: John Galliano at a Vogue World party in Paris in June 2024.

item.title: Who Designed Chris Fleming’s Purple ‘Live at the Palace’ Jumpsuit?
item.description: Anthony Sartino, who also outfitted Prince, has styled his first comedian and made him look like the rock star he is.
item.dc:creator: Kasia Pilat

item.title: From ‘Traitors’ to the Oscars Red Carpet, Men’s Overalls Are Getting Exposure
item.description: The work wear and children’s staple has lately become associated with the images of notable men.
item.dc:creator: Max Berlinger

item.title: Vogue Publisher Condé Nast Sues Dogue Magazine, a Home for Dog Fashion
item.description: The creator of Dogue, a small canine fashion magazine (circulation: under 100), has been accused of trademark infringement by Vogue’s publisher, Condé Nast.
item.dc:creator: Callie Holtermann
item.media:credit: Gabriella Angotti-Jones for The New York Times

item.title: Elton John’s Oscars Watch Party Attracts Alysa Liu, Dua Lipa and More
item.description: At his annual fund-raiser, the rock star hosted Keke Palmer, Donatella Versace and Brooklyn Beckham for a private Oscars watch party in West Hollywood.
item.dc:creator: Callie Holtermann and Krista Schlueter
item.media:credit: Krista Schlueter for The New York Times

item.title: The Best Oscars Fashion Trend Was Dresses You Can Breathe In
item.description: It was the best trend on the Oscars red carpet.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman
item.media:credit: Jutharat Pinyodoonyachet for The New York Times
item.media:description: A different way to dress: Renate Reinsve in a strapless Louis Vuitton column.

item.title: Chase Infiniti Among Several Stars to Embrace Subtle Makeup at 2026 Oscars
item.description: On the Oscars red carpet, many celebrities opted for subtle pops of color over dramatic, high-glam makeup.
item.dc:creator: Alisha Haridasani Gupta
item.media:credit: Jutharat Pinyodoonyachet for The New York Times
item.media:description: Chase Infiniti’s subtle makeup matched her custom Louis Vuitton lilac gown.

item.title: Anna Wintour and Anne Hathaway Riff on ‘The Devil Wears Prada’ at the Oscars
item.description: The Vogue empress presented alongside Anne Hathaway on Sunday evening for a not-so-subtle riff on “The Devil Wears Prada.”
item.dc:creator: Jacob Gallagher
item.media:credit: Philip Cheung for The New York Times
item.media:description: Borrowing a joke from the beloved fashion world sendup, Anna Wintour called Anne Hathaway “Emily" at one point as they presented the awards for best costume design and best makeup and hairstyling.

item.title: Oscars 2026 Red Carpet Photos: Timothée Chalamet, Teyana Taylor and More
item.description: See what nominees and guests for the 98th Academy Awards — including Teyana Taylor, Timothée Chalamet and Emma Stone — wore to the ceremony.
item.dc:creator: The Styles Desk
item.media:credit: Jutharat Pinyodoonyachet for The New York Times
item.media:description: Teyana Taylor in Chanel. She was nominated for best supporting actress for “One Battle After Another.”

item.title: Tiffany & Co Taps Natalie Portman, Mona Fastvold for New Campaign
item.description: Mona Fastvold has become associated with visually rich feature films and shorter videos for fashion brands. How does she square art with commerce?
item.dc:creator: Yola Mzizi

item.title: How to Watch the Oscars 2026: Start Time, Host and Streaming
item.description: Conan O’Brien is back for a second year as the host of the annual awards, which will again be available to stream on Hulu.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Bahr
item.media:credit: Jutharat Pinyodoonyachet for The New York Times

item.title: Tucker Carlson’s Merch Finds a Fan Base Outside of a Conservative Audience
item.description: Hats and shirts with provocative, internet-y slogans have found a place with people outside the conservative pundit’s constituency.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Gallagher and Nathan Taylor Pemberton

item.title: The Man Behind the Oscars ‘Glambot’ on the Red Carpet
item.description: Cole Walliser grabs celebrities for red-carpet close-ups. In recent years, he has become known in his own right, for better or for worse.
item.dc:creator: Madison Malone Kircher
item.media:credit: Michelle Groskopf for The New York Times

item.title: A Daughter Finds a Real ‘Love Story’ in Her Mother’s Closet
item.description: Calvin Klein from the 1990s is trending. Good thing Kara Mendelsohn saved it all from when she worked there.
item.dc:creator: Alyssa Vingan
item.category: Love Story: John F. Kennedy Jr. & Carolyn Bessette (TV Program)
item.media:credit: Eleanor Schmitt for The New York Times
item.media:description: Kara Mendelsohn with her daughter, Ella, who wears an outfit like one Carolyn Bessette wore in a famous photograph alongside John F. Kennedy Jr.

item.title: A New Magazine? In This Media Environment?
item.description: Now Voyager, an ambitious publication packed with dispatches from around the world, throws itself a party in Harlem.
item.dc:creator: Alex Vadukul
item.media:credit: Rebecca Smeyne for The New York Times
item.media:description: The $29 debut issue, filled with international dispatches, and the totes to go with it.

item.title: Margareta Magnusson, 91, Dies; Wrote of Cleaning Up Before Dying
item.description: Her best-selling book encouraged people to tidy up homes and lives as death approached — as a gift for loved ones and to revisit memories.
item.dc:creator: Chris Kornelis
item.category: The Gentle Art of Swedish Death Cleaning: How to Make Your Loved Ones' Lives Easier and Your Own Life More Pleasant (Book)

item.title: Overlooked No More: Eleanor Abbott, the Creator of Candy Land
item.description: She invented the game nearly 80 years ago to distract children who were suffering in the hospital during a polio outbreak.
item.dc:creator: Sandra A. Miller and Margaret Muirhead

item.title: Tracing the Sweet Origins of Candy Land and Its Creator
item.description: The writers of an Overlooked obituary about the game’s creator recount how they stumbled upon her and then proceeded to reconstruct her life.
item.dc:creator: Sandra A. Miller and Margaret Muirhead

item.title: Yes, He Actually Tried to Woo Me by Drone
item.description: My Nigerian tech bro considered flowers to be “too analog.”
item.dc:creator: Solape Adetutu Adeyemi
item.media:credit: Brian Rea

item.title: It Took a Village (and a Parents’ Group and a Knitting Group) to Get Married
item.description: A blizzard forced Priscilla Villalobos and Timothy Kolk to rethink their New York wedding, and find a new officiant — fast.
item.dc:creator: Sadiba Hasan

item.title: When It Came to Their Vows, Rapper Remy Banks and Ashley Condina Didn’t Miss a Beat
item.description: At their Valentine’s Day wedding, Mr. Banks read the verses of a song he wrote for his girlfriend of about six years, the artist Ashley Condina.
item.dc:creator: Tammy LaGorce

item.title: The Amtrak Ticket That Kept Getting Extended
item.description: Richard Hudock and Justin Goldman talked for about eight hours the night they met at a rooftop party. They have barely stopped since.
item.dc:creator: Shivani Vora

item.title: He Loves Crosswords. She Wrote the Book on Them.
item.description: Adrienne Raphel and David Rosenberg had little luck in dating — until a riff about animals and Italian food brought them together.
item.dc:creator: Tammy LaGorce

item.title: The Jewelry Industry Is Taking a Wild Ride as Gold and Silver Prices Soar
item.description: Some jewelers are turning to wood, steel and even platinum. Others are leaning into the volatility with bigger and bolder designs.
item.dc:creator: Victoria Gomelsky
item.media:credit: Marcos Chin

item.title: Remembering Marilyn Monroe, Draped In Diamonds and Coveting More
item.description: Events marking the birth centennial of the person who became Marilyn Monroe will feature jewelry — though she was rarely associated with expensive pieces offscreen.
item.dc:creator: David Belcher
item.media:credit: Michael Ochs Archives/Getty Images
item.media:description: Marilyn Monroe, wearing lots of diamonds, in a publicity photo for “Gentlemen Prefer Blondes” in which she sang “Diamonds Are a Girl’s Best Friend” wearing the same outfit and jewelry.

item.title: Flowers Are Blooming in California’s Death Valley
item.description: Visitors are flocking to see a bonanza of wildflowers that has transformed this barren desert.
item.dc:creator: Amy Graff and Gabriela Bhaskar

item.title: What’s With All the Celebrities Walking the Runways?
item.description: At Paris Fashion Week, niche celebrities and looksmaxxing were all over the runway.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Gallagher
item.media:credit: Clockwise from top, Tom Nicholson/Associated Press; Miu Miu; Antoine Flament/Getty Images
item.media:description: Teach you how to stunt: Gillian Anderson walking in Miu Miu, Chloë Sevigny at Miu Miu, too, and Marilyn Manson opening Enfants Riches Déprimés.

item.title: My Sister Doesn’t Want to Spend Time With My Kids
item.description: Our Ask the Therapist columnist, Lori Gottlieb, advises a reader whose sibling moved away and then canceled a family trip.
item.dc:creator: Lori Gottlieb
item.media:credit: Marta Monteiro

item.title: What Zendaya and Others Wore on the Streets at Paris Fashion Week
item.description: On and off the runways, ensembles at Paris Fashion Week married extravagant flourishes with a carefree sensibility.
item.dc:creator: Simbarashe Cha
item.media:description: Scarves, like the one worn knotted at the neck by the model Delilah Koch, were a staple at Paris Fashion Week.

item.title: Why Interior Design Magazines Are Booming
item.description: How the magazine world is changing to meet a new generation of décor enthusiasts.
item.dc:creator: Alice Cavanagh
item.category: Holiday International Interiors and Gardens Review (Magazine)
